UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the Fé & Finanças application, storing their profile information. This entity does not store authentication credentials, assuming an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

IncomeEntry.title: IncomeEntry
IncomeEntry.description: Represents a single monthly income entry recorded by a user, along with its calculated tithe amount.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IncomeEntry entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this income entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N IncomeEntry)

amount.description: The total income amount for this entry.

entryDate.description: The specific date associated with this income entry. This could be the date of receipt or a chosen date for the month.

description.description: An optional description or source detailing this income entry.

calculatedTithe.description: The calculated 10% tithe amount derived from the 'amount' for this entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the income entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the income entry was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must match the `request.auth.uid` for security. The `id` field within the document also matches the `userId` from the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Stores individual income entries for a specific user. The `userId` in the path and the `userId` field within the document must match, ensuring authorization independence and clear ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this income entry, corresponding to the parent UserProfile.

params.name: incomeEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific income entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for 'Fé & Finanças' prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization using a hierarchical, user-centric model. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored at `/users/{userId}`. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). This means ownership of a user's profile is inherently defined by the path itself. The `id` field within the UserProfile document will explicitly match the `userId` from the path.
2.  **IncomeEntry:** Income entries are stored as subcollections under their respective UserProfile: `/users/{userId}/incomeEntries/{incomeEntryId}`. This design is critical for authorization independence. The `userId` in the path (`{userId}`) explicitly identifies the owner of the `IncomeEntry` documents within that subcollection. Furthermore, the `userId` field *within* each `IncomeEntry` document is denormalized to match the `userId` in its parent path. This ensures that security rules do not need to perform expensive `get()` operations on the parent `UserProfile` document to determine ownership. All authorization checks can be performed directly on the `request.auth.uid` against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path and the denormalized `userId` field within the document, making rule evaluation atomic and efficient.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure inherently supports Querying All Possible (QAP) operations. For example, when a user wants to view their income history, they will query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/incomeEntries`. Firestore security rules can then easily ensure that `request.auth.uid == userId` for all read/write operations on documents within `/users/{userId}` and `/users/{userId}/incomeEntries`. This prevents unauthorized users from accessing or listing other users' data, as any query for a `userId` not matching `request.auth.uid` would be denied. The data retrieved is guaranteed to belong to the requesting user, eliminating the need for client-side filtering or complex, inefficient server-side filtering within rules.
